_metadata.title: Indian Parliament & Global Legislature — Verified Fact Database
_metadata.methodology: Every fact has been verified against a primary source. Where two primary sources (e.g., PRS and PIB) disagree, BOTH are recorded with the discrepancy noted. Facts without a verifiable primary source URL have been REMOVED. The 'quote' field contains the exact language from the source.
primary_sources_used: PRS Legislative Research (prsindia.org) — Vital Stats reports for each session
Press Information Bureau, Government of India (pib.gov.in) — Official press releases
UK Parliament (parliament.uk) — Standing Orders, FAQs, recess dates
Wikipedia — only for data that cross-references official sources
Parliament of Australia (aph.gov.au) — Official practice documents

india_2025_budget_session.quote: The Budget Session of Parliament was held between January 31 and April 4, 2025.
Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha functioned for 26 days.
Lok Sabha functioned for 111% of its scheduled time
india_2025_budget_session.discrepancy: PIB Press Release states 118%. PRS and PIB use different calculation methodologies.

india_2025_budget_session.source: Press Information Bureau, Government of India
india_2025_budget_session.source_title: Budget Session, 2025 of Parliament adjourns sine-die
india_2025_budget_session.quote: The productivity of Lok Sabha during the Budget Session, 2025 was approx. 118%
india_2025_budget_session.discrepancy: PRS reports 111%. PIB figure is higher.

india_2025_budget_session.source: Press Information Bureau, Government of India
india_2025_budget_session.quote: that of Rajya Sabha was approx. 119%
india_2025_budget_session.note: PRS Vital Stats page does not state RS % explicitly in the text accessed. PIB is the available source for RS.

india_2025_budget_session.quote: Question Hour functioned for 78% ... of its scheduled time in Lok Sabha
Question Hour functioned for ... 83% ... in ... Rajya Sabha respectively

india_2025_budget_session.fact: Starred questions answered orally Budget Session 2025
india_2025_budget_session.quote: Ministers gave oral response to 28% of starred questions in Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

india_2025_budget_session.fact: Waqf Amendment Bill debate time in LS
india_2025_budget_session.quote: On April 2, 2025, Lok Sabha discussed the Waqf (Amendment) Bill, 2024 for 13 hours and 53 minutes.

india_2025_budget_session.fact: Waqf Amendment Bill debate time in RS
india_2025_budget_session.quote: It discussed the Waqf (Amendment) Bill, 2024 for 12 hours and 49 minutes

india_2025_budget_session.note_on_source: PRS Session Wrap states 'five Bills were introduced, three of which were passed'
india_2025_budget_session.source: PRS Legislative Research, Monthly Policy Review April 2025
india_2025_budget_session.quote: During the session, five Bills were introduced, three of which were passed.

india_2025_budget_session.fact: Bills passed by both houses Budget Session 2025
india_2025_budget_session.source: PRS Legislative Research, Monthly Policy Review April 2025
india_2025_budget_session.quote: The Session was held from January 31 to April 4, 2025. Parliament passed ten Bills
india_2025_budget_session.note: Includes 3 newly introduced + 7 pending from earlier sessions.

india_2025_budget_session.fact: Income Tax Bill 2025 referred to Select Committee
india_2025_budget_session.source: PRS Legislative Research, Monthly Policy Review April 2025
india_2025_budget_session.quote: The Income Tax Bill, 2025 was introduced and referred to a Select Committee of Lok Sabha.
the first part of the Budget Session yielded a total of 9 sittings ... In the second part of the Session, there were 17 sittings

india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: The Monsoon Session of Parliament was held between July 21 and August 21, 2025.
Both Houses functioned for 21 days as originally scheduled.
Lok Sabha functioned for 29% of its scheduled time
the productivity of Lok Sabha has been approximately 31%
Rajya Sabha for 34%
that of Rajya Sabha approximately 39 %
two-thirds of the planned time was lost to disruptions
Lok Sabha worked for 23% ... of the scheduled Question Hour
Rajya Sabha worked for 6% of the scheduled Question Hour

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Starred questions answered orally LS Monsoon 2025
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: Ministers gave oral response to 8% of starred questions in Lok Sabha

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Starred questions answered orally RS Monsoon 2025
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: and 5% of starred questions in Rajya Sabha
13 Bills were introduced during the session (excluding appropriation Bills).
india_2025_monsoon_session.discrepancy: PIB states 14 bills introduced in Lok Sabha — likely includes an appropriation bill in the count.

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Bills referred to Joint Committees Monsoon 2025
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: Three were referred to Joint Committees

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Bills referred to Select Committees Monsoon 2025
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: two were referred to Select Committees of Lok Sabha

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Bills passed by both houses Monsoon 2025 (excluding finance/appropriation)
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: 14 Bills were passed by Both Houses (excluding finance and appropriation Bills).
india_2025_monsoon_session.discrepancy: PIB states 15 Bills passed by both Houses — likely includes appropriation bills.

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Income Tax Bill 2025 withdrawn, replaced by No. 2 Bill
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: The Bill was later withdrawn. Instead, a new Bill, the Income Tax (No.2) Bill, 2025 was introduced and passed on the same day in Lok Sabha, without any discussion.

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Income Tax (No.2) Bill LS time allocated vs spent
Regulation of Online Gaming Bill time in LS
india_2025_monsoon_session.note: Time allocated was not separately listed; 23 minutes in RS. Passed in 1 day.

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Operation Sindoor debate consumed 50% of LS functioning time
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: Operation Sindoor was discussed for about 19 hours in Lok Sabha and 16 hours in Rajya Sabha. It comprised about half the total functioning time of Lok Sabha and more than a third of the functioning time of Rajya Sabha.
the discussion engaged the Lok Sabha for 18 Hours 41 Minutes in which 73 Members took part and was replied to by the Prime Minister
In Rajya Sabha, the discussion engaged the House for a total time of 16 Hours 25 Minutes in which 65 Members took part
In Lok Sabha, discussions could happen for only 37 Hours out of total available 120 hours
in Rajya Sabha, discussions took place for 41 Hours 15 Mins only

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Private members' business not taken up
india_2025_monsoon_session.quote: No private members' business was held in either House during this session.
Lok Sabha continues to function without a Deputy Speaker – a Constitutional requirement – for over six years now.
This was the lowest functioning seen during the 18th Lok Sabha.

india_2025_monsoon_session.fact: Merchant Shipping Bill passed 244 days after introduction
india_2025_monsoon_session.note: 20 minutes discussion in LS, 10 minutes in RS. Time allocated: 2 hrs each house.

india_17th_lok_sabha.quote: The 17th Lok Sabha held its sessions between June 2019 and February 2024.
Lok Sabha functioned for 88% of its scheduled time

india_17th_lok_sabha.fact: 17th LS avg sitting days per year
india_17th_lok_sabha.quote: The 17th Lok Sabha (2019-2024) averaged only 55 days a year—the lowest among all full-term Lok Sabhas.

india_17th_lok_sabha.source: Drishti IAS, citing PRS Legislative Research
india_17th_lok_sabha.quote: In the 17th Lok Sabha, only 10% of Bills were referred to committees, a sharp drop from the 14th LS (60%), 15th (71%), and 16th (25%), with just 14 Bills reviewed.

india_17th_lok_sabha.source: The Week, citing PRS Legislative Research
india_17th_lok_sabha.quote: Question Hour functioned for 60 per cent of scheduled time in Lok Sabha

india_17th_lok_sabha.source: The Week, citing PRS Legislative Research
india_17th_lok_sabha.quote: a mere 52 per cent in Rajya Sabha
11 of its 15 sessions were adjourned early

india_historical.fact: Avg sitting days in first two decades of Parliament
india_historical.value: a little more than 120 days a year
india_historical.quote: During the first two decades of Parliament, Lok Sabha met for an average of a little more than 120 days a year.

india_historical.fact: NCRWC recommendation for minimum sitting days
india_historical.value: at least 120 sitting days for the Lok Sabha and 100 for the Rajya Sabha
india_historical.quote: The National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution suggested at least 120 sitting days for the Lok Sabha and 100 for the Rajya Sabha.
only 14 have been passed, with the last one in 1970

india_historical.fact: India has no fixed parliamentary calendar
india_historical.quote: India does not have a fixed parliamentary calendar.

india_historical.fact: Committee referral rates across Lok Sabhas
india_historical.source: Drishti IAS, citing PRS Legislative Research
india_historical.quote: a sharp drop from the 14th LS (60%), 15th (71%), and 16th (25%)

global_uk.quote: There are 20 days allocated for this purpose per session (under Standing Order 14).

global_uk.value: 17 to Official Opposition, 3 to second largest party
global_uk.source: UK Parliament, MPs' Guide to Procedure
global_uk.quote: The Leader of the Opposition gets 17 days. The other three days are allocated to the third largest party in the House.

global_uk.source: UK Parliament, List of previous Commons Recess Dates
global_uk.quote: State Opening: 7 November 2023. Prorogation: 24 May 2024. Total number of sitting days: 101

global_uk.source: UK Parliament, List of previous Commons Recess Dates
global_uk.quote: State Opening: 10 May 2022. Prorogation: 26 October 2023. Total number of sitting days: 213

global_us.source: Wikipedia, List of acts of the 119th United States Congress
global_us.quote: The 119th United States Congress ... has enacted 81 public laws and two private laws.
global_us.note: Wikipedia article cites Congress.gov as underlying source. Count as of article access date.

global_germany.fact: Bundestag minimum sitting weeks per year
global_germany.quote: The Bundestag does not have sessions as such, but at least 20 sitting weeks a year

global_germany.fact: Bundestag sits for at least twenty weeks per year
global_germany.source: Wikipedia (Bundestag article), citing Bundestag official sources
global_germany.quote: Normally, the Bundestag sits for at least twenty weeks per year, interrupted by non-sessional weeks

global_australia.fact: Australia House of Reps average sitting days since 1901
global_australia.source: Parliament of Australia, official Practice document
global_australia.quote: Since 1901 the House has sat, on average, 67 days each year spread over 20 sitting weeks for a total of 627 hours per year.

global_canada.fact: Canada House of Commons typical sitting weeks per year
global_canada.quote: In practice, parliament typically sits for approximately 27 weeks per year.
In Canada there are twenty-two opposition days per parliamentary session.

global_france.value: First working day of October to last working day of June
global_france.quote: Parliament sits in one ordinary session which starts on the first working day of October and ends on the last working day of June.

global_japan.quote: regular, annual sessions of the National Diet ... They last for 150 days and can be extended once.

india_no_opposition_days.fact: India does not have formal Opposition Days
india_no_opposition_days.quote: In India, the Parliament does not have an opposition day, but one day every week (Friday) during a session is devoted to private members' bills and resolutions.
